title: Where DentalTechHub Should Focus Marketplace Innovation
bluf: Prioritize Job Steps 4 through 8 (Discover, Screen, Evaluate, Validate, Compare) for marketplace innovation.
thesis: The JTBD outcome analysis shows the buying journey's middle steps — discovering, screening, evaluating, validating, and comparing solutions — are where practices feel the most friction, making them the highest-opportunity steps for a marketplace to fix.

nodes.title: Prioritize Job Steps 4-8 for marketplace innovation
nodes.summary: Which job steps in the technology-selection journey DentalTechHub's marketplace should prioritize solving.
nodes.question: Which job steps should DentalTechHub prioritize for marketplace innovation?

sourceRefs.quote: DentalTechHub has the highest opportunity to create value in Job Steps 4-8

nodes.title: Discover step is high opportunity
nodes.summary: Job Step 4 (Discover) is a very-high-opportunity step because information is fragmented and sources are biased.

sourceRefs.quote: Fragmented information, biased sources, no central hub

nodes.title: Evaluate step is high opportunity
nodes.summary: Job Step 6 (Evaluate) is a very-high-opportunity step because pricing is hidden and information is hard to compare.

sourceRefs.quote: Hidden pricing, outdated info, hard to compare, fake reviews

nodes.title: Buyers want faster discovery
nodes.summary: A stated desired outcome for Job Step 4 is minimizing the time it takes to find relevant solutions.

sourceRefs.quote: Minimize the time it takes to find relevant solutions

nodes.title: Buyers want pricing transparency
nodes.summary: A stated desired outcome for Job Step 6 is minimizing hidden pricing or cost surprises.

sourceRefs.quote: Minimize hidden pricing or cost surprises

nodes.title: Job executors
nodes.summary: The core technology-selection job is performed by multiple executor roles across a practice or DSO.

sourceRefs.quote: Practice Owner, Office Manager, DSO Executive, DSO Procurement Specialist
